title: Stock Valuation Desk: Gordon Growth & CAPM
briefing: Avalon Equity Research covers the consumer discretionary sector. A portfolio manager has asked you to value Nova Consumer Brands (ticker: NCB), a fictional mid-cap company. NCB just paid a dividend of $2.40 per share and has grown dividends at 5% annually for the past 5 years. The risk-free rate is 4.0% and the market risk premium is 6.5%. NCB's beta is 1.20. Analysts expect dividend growth to continue at 5% for the next 3 years, then decelerate to 3.5% thereafter. Using the Gordon Growth Model (constant growth) and a two-stage model, estimate NCB's intrinsic value and compute the cost of equity using both CAPM and the dividend growth approach. Write a brief equity research note with your valuation and recommendation.
learningObjectives: Apply the Gordon Growth Model (constant growth) to value a stock
Value a stock using a two-stage dividend growth model
Compute cost of equity using CAPM
Compute cost of equity using the dividend growth approach
Compare intrinsic value to market price and make a recommendation
